title: Lose of connectivity and the ability to send/receive emails.
creator: tbrunhart
content: As of March 24, my Mozilla Thunderbird email seemingly cannot connect to the internet. Therefore, I have no ability to receive of send emails. I am a member of the Best Buy tech support capability and their technicians spent several hours trying to correct this issue and finally stated that I need to seek actual Mozilla Thunderbird technician support to correct the lack of connectivity.

title: Trying to access non existing account
creator: leotlion
content: Suddenly Thunderbird is trying to access a non existing icloud email account. I have never used icloud and no such account is listed in Thunderbird. Yet it keeps asking for the required password. Again, no such account, or password have ever been given to Thunderbird. Trying to disable icloud goes nowhere as it doesn't recognize any passwords. 115.18

title: clicking on, or hitting enter on a new email opens up a reply email window, not the original email only
creator: mail2624
content: I have three email accounts. On each of the accounts when I am in the in box, clicking on or hitting enter does not open up he email but instead opens of a reply to the sender in a new tab. This is new! I have looked on line and have tried all of the following except fixing the Folder Flag add on as I do not have that installed: In the Drafts and Templates section, I confirmed that the Drafts folder on: is set to your actual Drafts folder and not your Inbox (although the Drafts folder is in my inbox). I did try and create a Draft folder outside the Inbox but could not. I have set the value for mail.openMessageBehavior to 0. However every time I look at it it seems to be changed back to 2 The issues happens even in troubleshoot mode with add ons (I only have one) disabled I rebuilt the Global Database by renaming global-messages-db.sqlite and restarting Thunderbird. I cannot install FolderFlags extension as it is not compatible with Thunderbird 149

title: Can Not Delete E-Mails
creator: paulp575123
content:
Thunderbird Version: 137.0 Problem: I have 2 e-mails in my inbox that I can not delete. I have read them and then I tried to delete them using the delete key, they would not delete. Now when I access Thunderbird, the message pane on the right side of the screen is blank; no message content. The from/to/subject lines above the content space do have content. I tried deleting them by deleting the global-messages-db in my profile folder; and having Thunderbird rebuilt the file. Did not help. What next steps should I take to delete these 2 e-mails?

Thanks.
